[2026-01-09T12:16:14.000Z] Cologne (the city where I live) has always been very Catholic (very Christian). Before Christmas, we have Advent, a time for reflection. Then comes Christmas, when we commemorate the birth of Jesus on the winter solstice, because Jesus gave us light in the form of love for our neighbor. Christmas then lasts at least until January 6th, when the Three Wise Men came to Jesus. (The relics of the Three Wise Men are kept here in Cologne Cathedral.) In the past, our calendar therefore ended on December 24th and the new one began on January 6th. This time is still called "between the years."

Today, nobody knows about love for one's neighbor anymore; Advent is spent at Christmas markets drinking mulled wine. Christmas Eve (before Christmas, gifts are demanded), and otherwise, the time between the years is used here to not work and to overeat. (Apparently, this is also the case here at FedEx.)
Happy New Year!
Stay healthy and in funny mood
Alexander
